chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Kicks a Woman in Her Private Part, and She Claims That She No Longer Menstruates

text: 5335 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَا تَرَى فِي رَجُلٍ ضَرَبَ اِمْرَأَةً شَابَّةً عَلَى بَطْنِهَا فَعَقَرَ رَحِمَهَا وَ أَفْسَدَ طَمْثَهَا وَ ذَكَرَتْ أَنَّهُ قَدِ اِرْتَفَعَ طَمْثُهَا عَنْهَا لِذَلِكَ وَ قَدْ كَانَ طَمْثُهَا مُسْتَقِيماً قَالَ "يُنْتَظَرُ بِهَا سَنَةً فَإِنْ صَلَحَ رَحِمُهَا وَ عَادَ طَمْثُهَا إِلَى مَا كَانَ وَ إِلاَّ اُسْتُحْلِفَتْ وَ أُغْرِمَ ضَارِبُهَا ثُلُثَ دِيَتِهَا لِفَسَادِ رَحِمِهَا وَ اِرْتِفَاعِ طَمْثِهَا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5335 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"What is your ruling regarding a man who struck a young woman on her abdomen, causing damage to her womb and disrupting her menstruation? She reported that her menstruation had ceased due to this injury, whereas it had previously been regular."
Imam (as) said: "She should be observed for one year. If her womb recovers and her menstruation returns to normal, then there is no liability. But if it does not return, she is to take an oath, and the assailant must pay one-third of her diyyah (blood money) for the damage to her womb and the cessation of her menstruation."
